testCases.title: Account Menu - Purchasing Group Structure
testCases.notes: Signed in as Carlos Rodriguez (test-carlos.rodriguez-20260310@test-agent.com, BuildRight org). Account sidebar shows 'Purchasing' group header visible with all 4 required links (Dashboard, Orders, Lists, Saved for later), plus allowed additions Quote requests and Back-in-stock list. Purchasing items are in expected order. No console errors. Account dashboard loaded HTTP 200.

testCases.title: Account Menu - User Group Structure
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Precondition 'personal account (without org)' cannot be satisfied. milamuller2024@yahoo.com was redirected to /change-password (forced password-change flow, blocks dashboard access). mutykovaelena@gmail.com has org context 'Tech & Solutions Inc.' (is Organization maintainer). All other agent pool personal users share the same issue. Without a clean personal account, cannot validate 'Addresses' link visibility specific to personal users. Workaround: Addresses suppression for org users is already confirmed by AUTH-029/031/032 observations on Carlos (BuildRight) and Elena (Tech & Solutions) — both org users do not see Addresses link, which matches the expected business rule.

testCases.title: Account Menu - Group Headers Are Non-Clickable
testCases.notes: DOM inspection: all 4 group headers (Purchasing, Marketing, Corporate, User) are SPAN elements inside DIV.vc-widget__header-container, NOT wrapped in <a> tags, have no href, and computed cursor style is 'auto' (not 'pointer'). Real-user click on Purchasing group text: URL remained /account/dashboard. Real-user click on User group text: URL remained /account/dashboard. No console errors.

testCases.title: Account Menu - All Navigation Items Route Correctly
testCases.notes: 7 of 8 menu items route correctly for Elena Mutykova (Tech & Solutions Inc. Organization maintainer): Dashboard→/account/dashboard, Orders→/account/orders, Lists→/account/lists, Profile→/account/profile, Change password→/account/change-password, Saved credit cards→/account/saved-credit-cards — all HTTP 200. FAIL: clicking 'Saved for later' sidebar link redirects to /403 Access denied page. Failure_Signals explicitly list '404 or /error page after click' as failure criterion. Per reported preliminary bug: may be account-permission specific (Elena's Tech & Solutions Inc. contact lacks saved-for-later permission) or a storefront permission enforcement regression. Addresses link not tested (not visible for org user — per business rule, expected).
testCases.failedAssertion: [NAV] Click 'Saved for Later' under Purchasing → expected /account/saved-for-later, actual /403 (Access denied)

testCases.title: Account Menu - Active State Highlighting
testCases.notes: Real-user click on 'Orders' in sidebar Purchasing group → navigated to /account/orders. Computed-style inspection: Orders link has classes 'router-link-active router-link-exact-active vc-menu-item__inner--active', font-weight 700 (bold), background rgb(223, 228, 231) gray. All other sidebar links had font-weight 400 and white background (not highlighted). After clicking 'Profile' in User group: only Profile has --active modifier; Orders no longer highlighted. Only one menu item is active at a time. Active state updates correctly on each navigation.

testCases.title: Account Menu - Mobile Responsive Layout
testCases.notes: Viewport resized to 375x667. Sidebar auto-collapsed; hamburger 'Main menu' button appears in header (64x55 px ≥ 44x44 touch target). Real-user click on hamburger opens drawer navigation. Mobile drawer shows: user Elena Mutykova, Logout button, and Purchasing/Marketing/Corporate/User group buttons in collapsible list — group structure preserved on mobile. Real-user click on Purchasing group button expanded submenu showing all 6 items. Real-user click on Orders→navigated to /account/orders. Minor note: 2 header icon buttons are 36x44 (width below 44px ideal), rest of interactive elements meet 44x44. Not a functional failure; documented as minor a11y observation.

testCases.title: Members Page Display
testCases.notes: /company/members page loaded HTTP 200 with heading 'Company members'. Member table shows 3 initial members: John Smith (Organization maintainer, john.smith@company.com, Active), Lena Test (Organization employee, lenatestqa@mail.com, Active), Elena Mutykova / own account (Organization maintainer, mutykovaelena@gmail.com, Active). Columns present: Name, Role, Email, Active (status). Own account visible. Invite members button visible for Admin. No console errors.

testCases.title: Invite Member - Buyer Role
testCases.notes: Real-user click opened 'Invite member' dialog. Default role 'Organization employee' (Buyer equivalent) preselected. Email filled auth036buyer1776758600@test-agent.com via textbox. Real-user click on Send. Success notification shown: 'The invite has been successfully sent'. Dialog closed. Invited email row appeared in members list with status 'Invited'. All GraphQL mutations returned HTTP 200. Role selector exposes: Organization employee, Purchasing agent, Organization maintainer (minimum Buyer+Admin covered).

testCases.title: Invite Member - Admin Role
testCases.notes: Real-user click opened invite dialog. Real-user click on role combobox opened listbox with options: Organization employee / Purchasing agent / Organization maintainer. Real-user click on 'Organization maintainer' (Admin equivalent) selected it. Email auth037admin1776758700@test-agent.com typed. Send clicked. Success message 'The invite has been successfully sent' displayed. Admin invitation with maintainer role sent successfully. Acceptance flow and permission-scope verification out of scope (requires invitee sign-up).

testCases.title: Edit Member Role
testCases.notes: Real-user click on Actions menu for Lena Test (Organization employee). Edit role / Block user / Delete options exposed. Real-user click on Edit role opened 'Change role' dialog with 3 radio options. Real-user click on Organization maintainer radio. Save clicked. Lena Test row updated to 'Organization maintainer' immediately in list. Cleanup: reverted Lena back to 'Organization employee' via same flow. No mutation errors.

testCases.title: Block Member
testCases.notes: Real-user click on Actions for Lena Test (Active Organization employee). Real-user click on Block user exposed confirmation dialog 'Are you sure you want to block access of the user?' with Cancel/OK. Real-user click on OK. Lena Test row status changed to 'Blocked' (red minus icon). Actions menu for blocked member now shows 'Unblock user' in place of 'Block user'. Member list updated immediately. Login-rejection verification for blocked user not performed (Lena's password not in test credential pool); this is a known test-data limitation, not a product defect.

testCases.title: Unblock Member
testCases.notes: Real-user click on Actions menu for blocked Lena Test. Menu item 'Unblock user' visible (confirmed both via screenshot evidence 033-auth040-actions-menu.png and DOM inspection). Menu portal not exposed to Playwright ref API from snapshot tree (rendered outside row's accessibility subtree), so used Tab-Tab-Enter keyboard navigation to activate Unblock user. Unblock confirmation dialog 'Are you sure you want to unblock access of the user?' appeared. Real-user click on OK. Lena Test status returned to 'Active' in member list (green check icon). Data/history preserved. Login-success verification for unblocked user not performed (same credential limitation as AUTH-039).

testCases.title: Filter Members by Role
testCases.notes: Real-user click on Filters button opened side panel with Role checkboxes (Organization maintainer, Organization employee, Purchasing agent, Store administrator, Store manager) and Status checkboxes (Active, Invited, Blocked). Checked 'Organization employee' via real-user click. Apply button enabled. Real-user click on Apply → list filtered to 2 Organization employee rows (Lena Test Active, auth036buyer invite-sent). Applied chip 'Organization employee' displayed with Reset filters button. Real-user click on Reset filters → list restored to 5 rows (full member list).

testCases.title: Search Members
testCases.notes: Real-user type 'Lena' into 'Search by name, role or email' input, Enter pressed. Member list dynamically filtered to 1 matching row (Lena Test / Organization employee / lenatestqa@mail.com). Search matches name. Cleared field via Ctrl+A, Delete, Enter → full 5-row list restored. No-results state not explicitly triggered, but filter mechanism works as expected.

bugs.title: 'Saved for later' sidebar link redirects to /403 Access denied for Organization maintainer (Tech & Solutions Inc.)
bugs.failedAssertion: [NAV] Each menu item routes to its correct page — 'Saved for later' expected /account/saved-for-later, actual /403 page
bugs.stepsToReproduce:
1. Sign in as mutykovaelena@gmail.com (Tech & Solutions Inc. Organization maintainer).
2. Navigate to /account/dashboard.
3. In the sidebar Purchasing group, click 'Saved for later'.
4. Observe URL redirects to /403 (Access denied page).
bugs.expected: Clicking 'Saved for later' sidebar link (visible in both Carlos's BuildRight account and Elena's Tech & Solutions account) should navigate to /account/saved-for-later page and render the feature.
bugs.actual: URL redirected to https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com/403 with page title 'QA & 403 Access denied'.
bugs.notes: May be account-specific permission scope issue for Tech & Solutions Inc. contact. Recommend reproducing with Carlos (BuildRight) and other org-maintainer accounts across multiple orgs to determine if this is (a) a per-contact permission issue (legitimate 403) or (b) a storefront regression where sidebar exposes links the backend denies. If (a), menu should not show the link; if (b), the 403 is the bug. Either way, the current UX — visible link + 403 — violates the navigation integrity invariant in BL-AUTH-001.

errors: AUTH-030 BLOCKED: Agent user pool does not include a clean personal account (no org). milamuller2024@yahoo.com is flagged for forced password change (redirects to /change-password). mutykovaelena@gmail.com is a Tech & Solutions Inc. Organization maintainer. Recommend seeding a dedicated personal-only storefront user for slot-3 (current pool slot-3 b2b is Carlos/BuildRight; no dedicated personal account). As partial mitigation, observations from AUTH-029 and AUTH-031/032/035 on org accounts confirm: 'User' group with Profile, Change password, Saved credit cards is present; 'Addresses' is correctly absent for org users per BL-AUTH / ECL-14.3. The case-specific 'personal account with Addresses link' assertion remains unexecuted.

AUTH-039 / AUTH-040: Login-rejection and login-success verification steps for the blocked/unblocked member (Lena Test) were not performed because Lena's password is not provisioned in the agent test credential pool. UI state transitions (Block/Unblock and resulting status labels + available menu actions) were verified directly. To cover the login-side assertion in a future run, seed a test-agent member whose password is known.
